mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals an imminent labor market deterioration that reduces corporate profit expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high real sales of new autos and low federal funds rates indicate robust consumer purchasing power and cheap financing, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS is in the top quartile and FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, with a negative Rank IC over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which triggers immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO increases are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases during the recovery phase.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%, or the signal shows zero predictive power in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts confirms broad-based economic expansion across labor and construction sectors, which sustains corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and HOUST is followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in > 50% of observations, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in total nonfarm payrolls below 100,000 signals weakening labor demand that reduces consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the payroll decline, or the signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.
